title: Cochlearia rivulorum
taxonomy: Cochlearia rivulorum is classified in the family Brassicaceae, commonly known as the mustard or cabbage family. This family includes a wide variety of plants, such as broccoli, cauliflower, and arugula. Cochlearia rivulorum belongs to the genus Cochlearia, which comprises about 20 species of flowering plants. The genus Cochlearia is placed in the order Brassicales, a group of flowering plants that also includes the mustard family. The order Brassicales is part of the larger clade Rosids, which includes many other plant families.

intro: Cochlearia rivulorum, also known as Rivulet Cress, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Brassicaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Rivulet Cress include glucosinolates, isothiocyanates, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal preparations, Rivulet Cress is often used to make infusions, tinctures, and salves, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory infections.
